title: Market Brief — Friday, March 27, 2026
summary: LLMflation hit 21.41, meaning the cost of frontier-grade intelligence has collapsed 79% from GPT-4 launch baseline in just three years. DeepSeek V3.2 at $0.38/M output is now 158x cheaper than o3 Pro at $80/M, compressing the entire market toward commodity pricing at the bottom while OpenAI defends ultra-premium at the top.
paragraphs: LLMflation hit 21.41, meaning the cost of frontier-grade intelligence has collapsed 79% from GPT-4 launch baseline in just three years. DeepSeek V3.2 at $0.38/M output is now 158x cheaper than o3 Pro at $80/M, compressing the entire market toward commodity pricing at the bottom while OpenAI defends ultra-premium at the top.

DeepSeek and Meta are selling below TCO-adjusted production cost: Llama 70B on H100 costs roughly $5.36/M tokens after the 1.25x multiplier, meaning Meta's Maverick at $0.60/M and DeepSeek V3.2 at $0.38/M are burning cash to buy market share. OpenAI's o3 Pro prints money at $80/M output against sub-$7 TCO, while B200 operators running medium-tier pricing face negative 90% margins, making new GPU inventory a trap without premium contracts.

B200 spot at $7.81/hr with only 20% availability is overvalued given margins go negative at every price tier below premium. DeepSeek R1 at $2.50/M output on frontier-class reasoning is the best risk-adjusted value in the stack; accumulate inference allocation there and short B200 spot exposure until availability breaks above 35%.
bullets: 21.41 LLMflation index marks a 79% cost collapse from the GPT-4 era baseline of 100.
$0.38/M output from DeepSeek V3.2 is selling at an estimated loss of $4.98 per million tokens versus TCO-adjusted H100 cost.
18% H200 availability with a 5.3% day-over-day price spike signals tightening supply for mid-tier GPUs.
$80/M output on o3 Pro delivers roughly 90% gross margin, making it OpenAI's highest-margin product.
1.25x TCO-adjusted B200 cost hits $9.76/hr, pushing breakeven above premium tier for most inference workloads.
$0.60/M output on Llama 4 Maverick grades Meta as Burning Cash against a $5.36/M production floor.
